calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-02-2014 Off Calendar

Ntc Of Default Judgment Prove Up Hearing
Off Calendar Nov-26-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Donald Sullivan, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Sonia Wong, #11288.

John H Dresslar, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) USAA Federal Savings Bank ("USAA FSB").
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.

After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant(s) as follows:

IT IS HEREBY ORDERED ADJUDGED AND DECREED that the following Reconveyance recorded on May 1, 2009 in the San Francisco County Recorder's Office as Instrument Number 2009-I755382 is hereby cancelled nunc pro tunc and declared to be null and void ab inition with respect to real property commonly know as 85 Turquoise Way, San Francisco, CA, 94131, legally described:
See Scanned Judgment.
IT IS HEREBY ORDERED ADJUDGED AND DECREED that Plaintiff's Deed of Trust executed on November 21, 2007 and recorded on January 10, 2008 in the San Francisco County Recorder's Office as Intstrument number 2008-I5166733 be reinstated as a valid first priority, fully enforceable Deed of Trust against the Subject Property along with the debt it secures, and has been valid and enforceable as a first Priority security interest in the Subject Property at all times since January 10, 2008 up to and including the present.
IT IS HEREBY ORDERED ADJUDGED AND DECREED that USAA FSB's Deed of Trust executed on May 27, 2006 and recorded on June 28, 2006 in the San Francisco County Recorder's Office as Intrument Number 2006-I201349 shall be reconveyed and said reconveyance is effective as of January 9, 2008.
IT IS HEREBY ORDERED ADJUDGED AND DECREED that the Court specifically retains jurisdiction of the cause to make such additional orders as may be necessary to effectuate and enforce the provisions of this judgment.
Judgment signed in open court.
